pattern: When a == b, their XOR is 0, meaning the XOR over arr[i..k] is 0; every valid (i,k) pair contributes k-i triplets from the free choice of j.
hints: If a == b then a XOR b == 0, which means arr[i] XOR arr[i+1] XOR ... XOR arr[k] must equal 0.
Use a prefix XOR array so you can check any range XOR in O(1); then find all (i, k) pairs where the range XOR is zero.
For each valid (i, k) pair with zero range XOR, j can be any index from i+1 to k, giving k-i distinct triplets.

edge_inputs.description: two identical adjacent elements — one triplet
all ones — many pairs XOR to zero
all distinct values, no range XORs to zero
single element — no valid triplet possible
two different elements — neither XOR pair works
long run of same value yields many overlapping triplets
XOR cancellation requires carrying across multiple elements
